title: GB200 NVL72 featuring industry-leading direct liquid cooling now available - Technovera
text:
Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE: HPE) today announced the successful shipment of its first solution from the NVIDIA Blackwell family, the NVIDIA GB200 NVL72. This high-performance, rack-scale system is specifically engineered for service providers and large enterprises, enabling the rapid deployment of large-scale, complex AI clusters.
The NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 is equipped with cutting-edge direct liquid cooling technology, enhancing both efficiency and performance. This advanced cooling solution is designed to handle the demands of high-density AI workloads, helping organizations optimize power consumption and system reliability.
By offering a powerful and scalable solution, HPE continues to support enterprises in accelerating AI innovation and addressing the growing need for efficient, high-performance computing in data-driven industries.
HPE Delivers High-Performance AI Solution with NVIDIA GB200 NVL72
"AI service providers and large enterprise model builders are facing immense pressure to deliver scalability, extreme performance, and rapid time-to-deployment,” said Trish Damkroger, Senior Vice President and General Manager of HPC & AI Infrastructure Solutions at HPE. “With our leadership in building the world’s top three fastest systems featuring direct liquid cooling, HPE empowers customers with lower cost-per-token training and unparalleled performance, backed by our industry-leading services expertise."
The NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 leverages a shared-memory, low-latency architecture and the latest GPU technologies to handle extremely large AI models—up to a trillion parameters—all within a unified memory space. This solution integrates NVIDIA CPUs, GPUs, compute trays, networking, and software, delivering seamless performance for demanding, parallelized workloads like generative AI (GenAI) model training and inference, along with NVIDIA’s software applications.
“Engineers, scientists, and researchers need cutting-edge liquid cooling to meet the escalating power and compute demands of modern AI systems,” said Bob Pette, Vice President of Enterprise Platforms at NVIDIA. “Thanks to the ongoing collaboration between HPE and NVIDIA, the shipment of the GB200 NVL72 will provide service providers and large enterprises with an efficient, scalable solution for building and deploying large AI clusters.”
With escalating power requirements and data center density dynamics, HPE has five decades of liquid cooling expertise that uniquely positions the company to help customers bring fast deployment and an extensive infrastructure support system for complex liquid-cooled environments
With escalating power requirements and data center density dynamics, HPE has five decades of liquid cooling expertise that uniquely positions the company to help customers bring fast deployment and an extensive infrastructure support system for complex liquid-cooled environments. This experience has enabled HPE to deliver eight of the top 15 supercomputers on the Green500 list, which ranks the world’s most energy-efficient supercomputers. HPE is recognized as a leader in direct liquid cooling technology, having built seven of the top 10 world’s fastest supercomputers.
Features of NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 by HPE:
Industry Leading Services and Support:
HPE is able to deliver AI solutions at a global scale, with proven ability to support massive, custom AI clusters with superior serviceability including expert on-site support, customized services, sustainability services and more. HPC & AI Custom Support Services are tailored to meet customer needs. With several levels of SLA coverage, HPE provides enhanced incident management with proactive support through dedicated remote engineers, ensuring rapid installation and faster time-to-value. Available services include:
The newly shipped NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 by HPE is one of a wide array of high-performance computing and supercomputing systems that address every use case for GenAI, scientific discovery, and other compute-intensive workloads. Learn more about our compute and supercomputing systems and other solutions in the NVIDIA AI Computing by HPE portfolio.
As Technovera Co., we officially partner with well-known vendors in the IT industry to provide solutions tailored to our customers’ needs. Technovera makes the purchase and guarantee of all these vendors, as well as the installation and configuration of the specified hardware and software.
GB200 NVL72 featuring industry-leading direct liquid cooling now available
